holding_period_lt_under_5yr.description: Fraction of net LT gains held 1-5 years (vs 5+ years). Higher-income filers tend to hold longer.
sources: JCT estimates of holding period distributions
holding_period_lt_under_5yr.note: Values are calibration placeholders. Reform targets gains held <5 years for ordinary rate treatment.

gross_loss_ratio.description: Fraction of gross LT gains offset by harvested losses. If 0.85, only 15% of gross gains survive as net — the rest are deliberately sold losers used to offset.
gross_loss_ratio.note: Most investors with meaningful portfolios engage in year-end tax-loss harvesting, selling losers to offset winners. Higher-income filers harvest more aggressively (larger portfolios, better advisors, stronger incentive at higher marginal rates). Under reform, these stranded losses can no longer offset reclassified gains ($3K/yr cap).
